executive_summary: Today's idiosyncratic moves are dominated by a consumer staples logistics cluster centered on UPS (-11.9%, 7.6σ), which is driving significant contagion through causal links to KO (-6.0%, 7.4σ), PEP (-7.2%, 7.3σ), and PG (-3.2%, 3.8σ) with 0.44 cluster coherence. This represents a shared supply chain cost shock—likely tariff-impacted logistics costs or volume guidance concerns—not company-specific issues. The highest-risk signal is BLK's -7.9% (6.3σ) singleton move with zero news, requiring immediate investigation for undisclosed material events. BA's +4.8% (2.7σ) unexplained gain suggests a pending positive catalyst, warranting monitoring for disclosure. Immediate actions: investigate BLK for hidden catalyst, determine UPS cluster driver (company vs sector-wide), and reduce exposure to high-sensitivity consumer staples (PEP, KO) until clarity emerges.

alerts.title: UPS -11.9% idiosyncratic collapse triggers consumer staples supply chain contagion
alerts.summary: UPS suffered a -11.9% idiosyncratic move (7.6 sigma), serving as the epicenter for a consumer staples cluster collapse. alerts.title: KO -6.0% idiosyncratic drop linked to UPS logistics cluster shock
alerts.summary: Coca-Cola's -6.0% idiosyncratic residual (7.4 sigma) is causally linked to the UPS-centered cluster, with strong correlation to PEP (p=0.59) and PG (p=0.54). This is NOT a beverage-specific demand issue but rather a shared logistics/distribution cost shock. The magnitude suggests either: (1) material exposure to UPS rate increases, (2) tariff impacts on aluminum/packaging distribution, or (3) volume guidance concerns tied to retail logistics disruptions. The simultaneous move with PEP indicates a beverage subsector supply chain event.

alerts.title: PEP -7.2% idiosyncratic decline in UPS-driven consumer staples logistics cluster
alerts.summary: PepsiCo's -7.2% idiosyncratic move (7.3 sigma) sits at the center of the consumer staples logistics cluster, with causal links from both UPS (p=0.33) and KO (p=0.59), and driving PG (p=0.41). The magnitude between KO and PEP suggests a beverage-specific supply chain shock, potentially related to Frito-Lay distribution network costs or DSD (direct store delivery) system pressures. This is the most interconnected node after UPS, indicating PEP may have the highest sensitivity to logistics cost inflation in the cluster.

root_cause.explanation: PepsiCo's -7.2% idiosyncratic move (7.3 sigma) sits at the center of the consumer staples logistics cluster, with causal links from both UPS (p=0.33) and KO (p=0.59), and driving PG (p=0.41). The magnitude between KO and PEP suggests a beverage-specific supply chain shock, potentially related to Frito-Lay distribution network costs or DSD (direct store delivery) system pressures. This is the most interconnected node after UPS, indicating PEP may have the highest sensitivity to logistics cost inflation in the cluster.

recommended_actions.target: PEP long exposure, consider tactical short vs KO if PEP has higher logistics cost structure
recommended_actions.rationale: Highest interconnectedness in cluster suggests maximum vulnerability to logistics cost cascade

recommended_actions.target: PEP DSD network costs and Frito-Lay distribution margin structure
recommended_actions.rationale: 7.3 sigma move with central cluster position indicates material undisclosed cost pressure

alerts.title: PG -3.2% idiosyncratic move completes consumer staples logistics cluster
alerts.summary: Procter & Gamble's -3.2% idiosyncratic residual (3.8 sigma) is the smallest but still significant move in the cluster, with causal links from UPS (p=0.35), KO (p=0.54), and PEP (p=0.41). As a household products company, PG's inclusion confirms this is a broad consumer staples distribution shock, not beverage-specific. The lower magnitude suggests either lower UPS shipping concentration or better hedged logistics costs. This move validates the cluster hypothesis: a shared supply chain event affecting high-volume, low-margin consumer staples distribution.

sector_summary.Consumer Staples - Logistics Cluster: SEVERE IDIOSYNCRATIC SHOCK: UPS -11.9% (7.6σ) is driving a consumer staples supply chain contagion affecting KO (-6.0%, 7.4σ), PEP (-7.2%, 7.3σ), and PG (-3.2%, 3.8σ) through causal links with 0.44 cluster coherence. This is NOT a demand-side issue but a shared logistics/distribution cost shock, likely driven by UPS guidance on tariff-impacted shipping costs, last-mile delivery inflation, or volume warnings. The causal structure (UPS→PEP, UPS→PG, KO↔PEP) indicates a common supply chain vulnerability across high-volume, low-margin consumer staples. PEP shows highest interconnectedness and vulnerability; PG's lower magnitude may indicate better cost management. CRITICAL: Investigate UPS earnings guidance and tariff exposure immediately. Expect potential Q1 guidance revisions from PEP/KO if logistics cost inflation is structural. Reduce exposure to highest-sensitivity names (PEP, KO) until catalyst clarity.
